zh.lifeSummary: 當Sir John Ashton於1423年出生在英國藍卡爾阿希頓-德-林時，他的父親John Thomas de Ashton20歲，母親Elizabeth de Byron26歲。他於1439年之前與Dulcia Trafford在英國藍卡爾結婚。他們至少是1個兒子的父母。他去世於1484年享年61歲。

nameMeanings.name: Ashton
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English: habitational name from any of numerous places so called, especially Ashton-under-Lyne near Manchester. Most are named from Old English æsc ‘ash tree’ + tūn ‘settlement’; the one in Northamptonshire is (æt thǣm) æscum ‘(at the) ash trees’. Others have been assimilated to this from different sources. The one in Devon is ‘the settlement (tūn) of Æschere’, while the one in Hertfordshire is ‘the settlement of Ælli’.

variants.name: Aspden
meanings: English: habitational name from Aspden (alias Aspen) in Oswaldtwistle, Lancashire. This surname has been confused with   Ashton   and   Austin  , and probably also with   Aston   and   Astin  , all of which are common surnames in Lancashire.

nameMeanings.name: John
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.

historicEvents.title: Wars of the Roses
historicEvents.description: Wars of the Roses was a series of English civil wars for control of the throne of England between the royal families House of Lancaster and the House of York. The name of the war was derived as the red rose was the symbol of the Lancastrians and the white rose was the symbol of the Yorks.

en.lifeSummary: When Sir John Ashton was born in 1423, in Ashton-under-Lyne, Lancashire, England, his father, John Thomas de Ashton, was 20 and his mother, Elizabeth de Byron, was 26. He married Dulcia Trafford before 1439, in Lancashire, England. They were the parents of at least 1 son. He died in 1484, at the age of 61.
